The launch lands amid an EF researcher exodus of at least eight senior departures in 2026, with anchor funding from Bitmine, Sharplink, and Consensys founder Joe Lubin.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: Andrew Cuomo will co-chair the OKX-ICE crypto joint venture alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland, the companies disclosed Monday, marking the highest-profile political appointment yet in ICE's crypto push. The venture, formed alongside ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market.

title: Bitmine, SharpLink and Joe Lubin back Ethlabs nonprofit to advance Ethereum’s ‘next phase’ of growth
blurb: Bitmine and SharpLink, the two largest ETH treasury companies, joined Consensys founder Joe Lubin to back Ethlabs, a new nonprofit focused on driving institutional adoption of Ethereum. The group frames the effort as Ethereum's "next phase" of growth, aligning the largest corporate ETH holders behind a coordinated push to court banks and asset managers onto the network.

title: Anchorage aims to bring banks onchain with new tokenized deposit platform
blurb: Anchorage Digital launched a platform that lets banks issue and manage tokenized deposits, providing the blockchain infrastructure, wallet management, and smart contracts while banks retain custody and customer relationships. The federally chartered crypto bank is positioning the product as a parallel settlement layer for 24/7 payments, joining JPMorgan, Citi, and BitGo in betting that tokenized deposits, not stablecoins, will move commercial bank money onchain.

title: a16z-Backed Goldfinch Finance Winds Down After Originating $100M in Loans
blurb: Goldfinch Finance, the a16z- and Coinbase Ventures-backed onchain private credit protocol, is winding down via governance proposal GIP-87 after six years and roughly $100M in originated loans, with depositors reporting more than $50M stranded across eight borrowers, two in default and six in restructuring. Warbler Labs will shift Goldfinch into maintenance mode to collect remaining legacy payments, having spent $7M of its own funds repaying lenders amid weak demand for onchain private credit.

title: WSJ: $1.9M in Fake Bets Propped Up Polymarket Creator Videos
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that roughly $1.9M in bets shown across more than 1,100 creator videos promoting Polymarket were fabricated, with creators filmed placing trades on dummy versions of the platform, including one at the misspelled domain "poiymarket.com." The disclosure lands as Polymarket pursues U.S. re-entry through its CFTC-licensed exchange and courts institutional backing, raising fresh scrutiny of a platform that already settled with the CFTC for $1.4M in 2022.

title: 1,200 Tech Companies Tell Senate to Pass CLARITY Act as Lummis Calls Code-as-Crime Risk an 'Absurdity'
blurb: The Consumer Technology Association, whose 1,200 members include Amazon, Apple and Google, sent a June 17 letter urging Senate leaders Thune and Schumer to bring the CLARITY Act to the floor and pass it without delay. The push marks the fourth constituency letter in three weeks and broadens the bill's backing beyond crypto-native firms, with Senator Cynthia Lummis warning that missing the current window could push market-structure legislation to 2030.

title: Solana treasury firm Solmate’s largest stakeholder sues board for self-dealing, fiduciary breaches
blurb: Solmate's largest stakeholder sued the Solana treasury firm's board, alleging self-dealing and fiduciary breaches after directors Ron Sade and Keren Maimon personally purchased roughly 2.298 million SLMT shares at $4.97. The suit claims the insider buy diluted existing shareholders by about 20%, adding governance risk to a crypto-treasury vehicle already exposed to SOL price volatility.

title: HIVE stock surges 25% as Ivy League researchers train neural networks on Paraguay GPUs
blurb: Hive Digital Technologies (HIVE) jumped nearly 25% Monday to around $5.20, its highest in seven months, after a Columbia University team trained neural networks remotely on the bitcoin miner's Nvidia A40 GPU cluster in Paraguay and submitted the work to NeurIPS. The rally follows a three-year, $220M AI cloud contract with Bell Canada and Cohere expected to add roughly $70M in annual recurring revenue.

title: ENS DAO Opens Temp Check on Handing Treasury and Day-to-Day Authority to ENS Foundation
blurb: The ENS DAO opened a temp-check proposal to transfer treasury control, grants administration, and long-term capital strategy to an expanded five-seat ENS Foundation board, while tokenholders retain protocol-layer authority and the power to remove directors. Material funding decisions would require four of five director votes, and the Foundation would be barred from voting, lending, or delegating any ENS tokens it holds.

title: Dormant Wallet Tied to HashFlare Fraud Moves 10,600 ETH Worth $18.5M
blurb: A wallet tied to the HashFlare cloud-mining fraud moved 10,600 ETH worth roughly $18.5M on Monday, its first activity in about three and a half years, after investigator ZachXBT flagged the transfer. The operator began converting the ether to Bitcoin through cross-chain instant swaps including Near Intents, matching the laundering pattern ZachXBT documented in recent runs like the KelpDAO exploit aftermath.

title: Strive says digital credit selloff was a liquidation event, not a credit crisis
blurb: Strive executives characterized last week's sharp selloff in bitcoin-backed digital credit products as a leverage liquidation event rather than a credit failure, after Strategy's STRC preferred vehicle dropped to $82.53 before recovering near $90.50 and Strive's SATA fell into the low $90s before rebounding to roughly $98.59. Chief Risk Officer Jeff Walton pointed to heavy volumes, including about $950M in STRC trading Thursday, as evidence of deep liquidity rather than underlying credit deterioration.

title: Ethereum Foundation talent exodus sparks fresh debate over leadership
blurb: Ethereum Foundation co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang stepped down to return to a research role, becoming at least the ninth senior departure in five months and reigniting debate over the Switzerland-based nonprofit's management. The exits coincide with the EF's new "CROPS" strategic framework, which supporters read as a reaffirmation of cypherpunk values and critics dismissed as failing to address execution concerns.

title: Ether’s biggest corporate holders back new Ethereum research hub
blurb: Five former Ethereum Foundation researchers launched Ethlabs, an independent nonprofit R&D organization backed by SharpLink Gaming and Bitmine Immersion, two of the largest corporate ETH treasury holders, alongside Consensys CEO Joe Lubin, Anchorage Digital, Octant, and SNZ. The launch follows a wave of senior departures from the Ethereum Foundation and reflects a shift toward a multi-node development model that spreads protocol research across independent organizations.

title: Stablecoin Issuer KYC Rules Proposed
blurb: US federal regulators including FinCEN, the OCC, the Federal Reserve, the FDIC, and the NCUA proposed rules requiring GENIUS-compliant stablecoin issuers like Circle to run bank-grade Customer Identification Programs, with 24/7 sanctions screening and the ability to freeze or burn flagged tokens. Because the mandate covers only primary on- and off-ramps, Fed Vice Chair Michael Barr warned that secondary-market purchases from pseudonymous addresses could bypass identity checks entirely.

title: MoonPay expands into back-office financial operations with AI accounting startup Entendre acquisition
blurb: MoonPay acquired Entendre, an AI accounting startup that automates reconciliations, treasury management, and month-end close for high-volume crypto and fintech firms, marking its fifth acquisition this year. The deal pushes MoonPay beyond its crypto-fiat gateway roots into back-office financial operations, adding existing Entendre clients like Polygon Labs, Thirdweb, and Babylon Labs as it builds out MoonPay Trade and MoonPay Institutional.

title: Gate CrossEx: An Exchange-Native Answer to the Institutional Capital Fragmentation Problem
blurb: Gate CrossEx exited beta after launching in October 2025, offering exchange-native prime brokerage that pools margin across five major centralized exchanges with internally settled, instant collateral movement instead of on-chain rebalancing. The design lets multi-venue desks net collateral across accounts and avoid liquidations caused by fragmented exchange plumbing during volatility, though durability depends on adding venues and managing the credit risk of intermediating competitor positions.

title: Crypto industry groups urge Congress to pass tax staking and mining bill unchanged
blurb: Three crypto industry groups, the Blockchain Association, the Crypto Council for Innovation, and The Digital Chamber, sent a letter to House Ways and Means leaders urging Congress to pass the Tax Clarity for Mining and Staking Act unchanged, which would defer taxation on mining and staking rewards until the assets are sold. The bill remains in committee and faces pushback, with the American Bankers Association arguing it shows favoritism toward crypto over other asset classes.

title: Bitcoin treasury Strive’s shares jump as company’s holdings near 20,000 BTC
blurb: Strive, the bitcoin treasury company founded by Vivek Ramaswamy, said it bought 759 BTC for about $50 million, lifting its total holdings to 19,864 coins and ranking it the seventh-largest publicly traded bitcoin treasury, ahead of SpaceX and Coinbase. Shares (Nasdaq: ASST) rose nearly 10% on the news, even as bitcoin trades around $65,000, roughly 50% below its late-2025 high near $125,000.

title: Re7 Labs Opens $223K USDC Compensation Pool for USR Exploit Victims
blurb: Re7 Labs opened a 223,000 USDC compensation pool for users whose positions in its Re7 USDC Base and Re7 USDC Core Mainnet vaults were caught in the March 23 Resolv Labs exploit, with pro-rata claims routed through the Merkl app. The payout follows Gauntlet's larger 4.37 million USDC settlement on June 4, leaving smaller Morpho curators kpk and 9summits as the remaining open files on the cleanup.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: B2C2's Cactus Raazi joined Lex's podcast to detail how the institutional market maker prices across 40+ exchanges for roughly 1,500 clients and moves about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues crypto market making splits between riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha, lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world, and introduces PENNY for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Securitize and tZERO clash over patents as race to bring Wall Street onchain heats up
blurb: Securitize filed suit in Delaware seeking a declaratory judgment that it does not infringe tZERO's patents covering tokenized-securities compliance, issuance, and trading infrastructure, a week after tZERO sent a cease-and-desist alleging its DS Protocol and Vault Registrar violate those patents. The fight pits two of the largest tokenization firms against each other as banks like BlackRock and JPMorgan push into a market Citi projects at $5T by 2030.

title: Crypto's second U.S. lobbying front — tax policy — sees industry push on mining, staking
blurb: Three leading U.S. crypto lobbying groups — the Blockchain Association, the Digital Chamber, and the Crypto Council for Innovation — urged the House Ways and Means Committee to advance Representative Mike Carey's Tax Clarity for Mining and Staking Act unchanged, which would let miners and stakers defer taxes on new assets until they sell rather than at receipt. Tax policy has become the industry's second lobbying priority behind the market structure bill still being debated in the Senate.

title: Polymarket's viral videos showed people winning big, but the bets were fake
blurb: A WSJ investigation found Polymarket paid creators to film fake winning bets on dummy versions of its site to recruit US users.

title: TurboFlow, aiming to be the ‘Kalshi of APAC,’ raises $6 million seed led by Pantera Capital
blurb: TurboFlow raised $6 million in a seed round led by Pantera Capital, with Susquehanna Crypto and Digital Currency Group participating, to build an onchain prediction-market and perpetual-futures platform targeting Asian users. Founded by former Amber Group partner Tony He and based in Hong Kong, the SAFE-with-token-warrants round closed in March as the platform positions itself as a localized alternative to Kalshi and Polymarket.

title: The PIK Fuse: How Private Equity Software Deals Like Medallia Actually Blow Up, and Exactly Who’s Next
blurb: Thoma Bravo handed Medallia to its lenders in April, wiping out roughly $5.1B in equity after buying the company for $6.4B in 2021. The trigger was a PIK toggle that let debt service compound to ~$300M a year against ~$200M in earnings, hiding the gap until the deferral window closed. The author argues a dozen more peak-era PE software deals are on the same fuse, with timing knowable because the pattern repeats every time.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the NYSE parent, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to tokenize NYSE-listed equities, co-chaired by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, giving OKX's customers access to ICE futures and tokenized NYSE equities, following ICE's March investment that valued OKX at $25B.

title: U.S. Senate passes housing bill that carries four-year ban on a Fed CBDC
blurb: The U.S. Senate passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act in an 85-5 vote, attaching a four-year ban on a Federal Reserve central bank digital currency that runs through the end of 2030. The provision is largely symbolic, since the Fed has no active CBDC project, but it formally closes the door on a digital dollar while the ECB pilots a digital euro and China expands its digital yuan.

title: Morgan Stanley Files Amended S-1s for Spot Solana and Ethereum Trusts
blurb: Morgan Stanley filed amended S-1s for spot Solana and Ethereum trusts, naming BNY Mellon and Coinbase Custody as joint custodians and setting a 0.14% sponsor fee with MSOL and MSSE listing on NYSE Arca. The fee matches the bank's bitcoin ETF MSBT and undercuts rival altcoin issuers, though Canary's Litecoin ETF has drawn just $9M in eight months, leaving institutional demand for single-asset altcoin wrappers unproven.

title: Intercontinental Exchange, OKX expand access to tokenized equities via joint venture co-chaired by former Gov. Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the NYSE parent, and crypto exchange OKX formed a 50-50 joint venture to give OKX's customers access to ICE futures and tokenized NYSE equities, pending regulatory approval. The venture will operate as a US-registered broker-dealer and FCM and is co-chaired by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo, extending an ICE-OKX tie-up that began with ICE's investment at a $25B valuation in March.

title: Investors get real-time view of UK bond market activity for the first time
blurb: The FCA launched a bond consolidated tape operated by ETS Connect UK, giving investors a single real-time view of UK bond prices and trading activity for the first time, with 98% market coverage. The launch builds on December 2025 transparency rules that lifted real-time reporting of corporate bond trades from under 5% to over 75%, and makes the UK the first market outside North America to consolidate bond data this way.

title: Bank of England Publishes Policy Statement and Draft Rules for Systemic Stablecoins
blurb: The Bank of England published a policy statement and draft Code of Practice for systemic sterling stablecoins, replacing proposed per-person holding caps with a £40B issuance ceiling per coin and a backing mix of up to 70% short-dated gilts plus at least 30% unremunerated central bank deposits. HM Treasury still designates which coins qualify as systemic, leaving non-systemic issuers under the FCA, with industry feedback open until September 22.

title: Meta buys stake in Cred, appoints founder to lead WhatsApp
blurb: Meta invested $900M for a 20% stake in India-based fintech Cred and named founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart. The move ties Meta's largest messaging platform to a consumer-payments operator as it pushes deeper into WhatsApp-based commerce and payments in India, its biggest market by users.

title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Supply Grows 9.7% in a Week to $4.85 Billion
blurb: World Liberty Financial's USD1 stablecoin added roughly $427 million over seven days, a 9.7% jump to $4.85 billion that makes it the fourth-largest dollar-pegged token behind Tether, USDC and Sky's USDS. The mint ran counter to USDS and PayPal's PYUSD, which both contracted over the same window, and traces to Aster routing its RWA perpetuals through USD1 and a UFC prize payout, though DefiLlama lists no public audit attestation for the reserves.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: OKX and Intercontinental Exchange named former New York governor Andrew Cuomo to co-chair their crypto joint venture, alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland. The venture, formed with ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX's spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE's futures and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market, pending regulatory approval.

title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Acquisition, Launches Franklin Crypto for Institutional Allocators
blurb: Franklin Templeton closed its acquisition of 250 Digital and launched Franklin Crypto, an active digital-asset unit targeting pensions, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional allocators, with part of the consideration paid in its on-chain BENJI tokens. The move gives the $1.78T manager three institutional crypto lines, layering active management onto its existing tokenized-treasury exposure, which has crossed $2.5B in AUM and roughly doubled year to date.

title: SpaceX Readies Its $20 Billion Bond Debut to Fund AI Ambition
blurb: SpaceX launched its first investment-grade dollar bond offering, a $20B debut expected to begin a sustained borrowing spree funding the company's AI ambitions after its record $75B IPO. The move signals SpaceX intends to finance compute buildout through debt markets rather than dilute equity further, putting a private space-and-AI issuer directly into competition with hyperscalers for investment-grade capital.

title: WhatsApp gets new chief as Meta taps India’s CRED founder Kunal Shah and invests $900M in startup
blurb: Meta led a $900M financing round in Indian fintech CRED, structured through primary and secondary shares, and named CRED founder Kunal Shah to run WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart. The move targets WhatsApp Pay's stalled growth in India, where it trails PhonePe and Google Pay despite WhatsApp's 500M-plus users, signaling Meta's intent to convert its largest market into a payments and commerce engine.

title: Anchorage aims to bring banks onchain with new tokenized deposit platform
blurb: Anchorage Digital launched a platform that lets banks issue and manage tokenized deposits, adding 24/7 settlement and payments without overhauling their core systems. The product mints onchain representations of customer deposits while the underlying funds stay in the bank's traditional accounts, positioning tokenized deposits as a bank-side alternative to stablecoins like USDC and USDT for moving money on blockchain rails.

title: MoonPay expands into back-office financial operations with AI accounting startup Entendre acquisition
blurb: MoonPay acquired Entendre, an AI accounting startup that automates reconciliations, treasury management, and month-end close for high-volume crypto and fintech businesses, in its fifth deal of the year. The purchase pushes MoonPay beyond its crypto-to-fiat gateway roots into the financial-operations layer, complementing recent moves like MoonPay Trade and MoonPay Institutional as it positions for stablecoin adoption at scale.

title: MSUSD drops 85% as proof-of-reserves provider terminates agreement
blurb: MSUSD, a yield token tied to MainStreet Finance, fell as much as 85% after Accountable terminated its proof-of-reserves verification agreement, pushing the Morpho msY/USDC lending market to 100% utilization. MainStreet says assets remain fully backed and blames a shuttered third-party dashboard rather than insolvency, citing over $8M USDC deployed for liquidity, but the depeg already exposed AlphaUSDC Delta V2 to roughly $18M in the affected market.

title: Bitcoin treasury Strive’s shares jump as company’s holdings near 20,000 BTC
blurb: Strive, the bitcoin treasury company founded by Vivek Ramaswamy, said it bought 759 BTC for roughly $50M, lifting its holdings to 19,864 coins and ranking it the seventh-largest publicly traded bitcoin treasury. Shares (Nasdaq: ASST) rose about 10% on the news, even as bitcoin trades near $65,000, down more than 50% from its late-2025 high and pressuring leveraged treasury firms toward consolidation.

title: Meta's WhatsApp head to step down, will be replaced by Indian fintech founder Kunal Shah
blurb: Meta named Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, replacing seven-year head Will Cathcart, and is investing $900M in his Indian fintech startup at a $4.5B post-money valuation. The appointment ties a credit-card payments operator to a 3-billion-user messaging platform that just began testing subscriptions, signaling Meta wants payments and commerce native to WhatsApp beyond ads.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: In this podcast, B2C2's Cactus Raazi explains how the institutional digital-asset market maker prices for roughly 1,500 clients across 40+ exchanges and underpins about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues that market makers aren't interchangeable, with riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha shops producing very different execution, and lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world and its PENNY product for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Rebuilding Securities Finance on Aave V4
blurb: Aave founder Stani Kulechov argues securities finance, a massive Wall Street market, is moving onchain via lending against securities collateral on Aave V4.

title: Yen Intervention Risk Rises on Reports of Katayama-Bessent Talks
blurb: The yen's slide toward fresh multi-decade lows has currency traders pricing in elevated odds of direct intervention, following reports of an online meeting between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. Coordinated or unilateral yen-buying by the Ministry of Finance would jolt cross-border settlement costs and FX hedging for any fintech moving money between dollar and yen rails.

title: Alan Greenspan has died
blurb: Alan Greenspan, who chaired the Federal Reserve from 1987 to 2006 and shaped US monetary policy across four presidencies, died at 100. His nineteen-year tenure spanned the 1987 crash, the dot-com boom, and the low-rate era that critics later tied to the 2008 financial crisis, leaving a legacy that still frames how central bankers weigh deregulation against systemic risk.
